{"key_event":"WWII mobilization and national-security state","reasoning":"World War II mobilization, mass taxation, wartime economic controls, and the 1947 National Security Act pushed an already strong federal state into near-overwhelming capacity, even after partial demobilization. Civil society also gained through labor strength, civil-rights organizing, veterans’ benefits, and middle-class institution-building, but internment, loyalty programs, anti-communism, and Taft-Hartley limited the net increase.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":8.1,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":9.3}
